title: setaria viridis subvar. lanceolata
intro: Setaria viridis subvar. lanceolata, also known as green foxtail or wild millet, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Setaria viridis subvar. lanceolata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
